docs.title: Security review 2026-05-06
docs.content:
Security Review 2026-05-06

Participants: carol, dave, eve

carol: We reviewed the Postgres RLS setup for multi-tenant isolation. Looks solid overall.
dave: The tenant isolation doc on Notion has the canonical GUC name. Worth cross-checking new migrations against it.
eve: Agreed. A few migration files we looked at today might be missing the FORCE RLS toggle for superuser queries.

docs.title: Tenant Isolation Design Doc
docs.content:
Tenant Isolation Design Doc

## Row-Level Security
All multi-tenant tables enforce RLS using the GUC app.current_customer_id. The helper function with_tenant sets this GUC before any query.

## Migration checklist
- Wrap Alembic UPDATEs in FORCE/NO FORCE RLS toggle for tables with RLS
- Always use app.current_customer_id (not app.tenant_id)

expected_edges.note: Security review meeting tangentially references the Notion tenant isolation doc as a cross-reference, without directly discussing it
